items.briefing:
Australians should be cautious of fake FIFA World Cup websites following an FBI warning about fraud schemes targeting fans ahead of the tournament. These sites are designed to steal personal and financial information, so only use official ticketing and event platforms. Separately, Carnival Cruise has confirmed a data breach affecting nearly six million people — if you or your family have used Carnival services, monitor your accounts and watch for suspicious emails or unusual activity.

Attackers are also using more subtle methods to compromise Microsoft 365 accounts, with Barracuda researchers flagging a shift toward stealthy techniques that can bypass standard security checks.

**Practical tip:** Before purchasing World Cup tickets or merchandise online, verify the website address carefully and stick to links provided through official sources.

featured.title: Certes says be ready to protect data before Q Day hits
featured.quote: Only 3% of Australian businesses prepared for post-quantum cryptography threats to sensitive data.
